summary:
The presence of antischkow cells is characteristic for most non-immune mediated disorders but not seen prominently in iron-deficiency anemia. Key differential diagnoses include sickle cell disease where such findings would be less common given its distinct pathophysiology involving chronic inflammation rather than direct nutritional deficiencies leading to cellular abnormalities similar those observed with antischkow cells formation.
The summary highlights that antischwartzman reaction-like phenomena occur more commonly outside pure hematological issues related directly by nutrition status as opposed to genetic polymorphisms causing structural blood component alterations typical among other listed options besides IDA [iron deficient anaemias]. 

---
